summary: Web Push feature tested. Backend: all 6 push tests PASS (VAPID key endpoint, subscribe/status/unsubscribe lifecycle with idempotent upsert, auth required, invalid-payload 400, employee CRUD + request creation still 2xx with push hooks in path). Frontend regression: app loads, owner login works, all 9 nav tabs render, SW remains 'activated', no console errors (excl. pre-existing Tailwind CDN warning). Push card + toggle render on Profil page with correct data-testid. Full subscribe-via-PushManager UI flow could NOT be exercised end-to-end because headless Chromium reports Notification.permission='denied' even after context.grant_permissions(['notifications']) — a known Playwright/headless quirk; the toggle correctly handles this denied state by showing 'Diblokir' + warning, which is correct app behavior. The subscribe/store/unsubscribe contract is fully validated via backend pytest with a fake subscription payload.

issues: Pre-existing Tailwind Play CDN warning — not push-related.

critical_code_review_comments: send_push_to_users() correctly short-circuits when VAPID not configured or no user_ids, and individual webpush failures are wrapped in try/except with 404/410 -> auto-cleanup of stale subscriptions. Good.
POST /api/push/subscribe uses upsert keyed on 'endpoint' (Mongo unique index on endpoint). Idempotent — verified by test: repeated subscribe does not duplicate count.
POST /api/push/unsubscribe scopes delete by both endpoint AND user_id so a user cannot remove another user's subscription. Good.
POST /api/push/subscribe raises 400 'Subscription tidak valid' when endpoint missing. Good input validation.
GET /api/push/vapid-public-key is unauthenticated (acceptable — public key is meant to be public).
push.js subscribePush() awaits navigator.serviceWorker.ready before pushManager.subscribe — correct.

profile.js push-toggle handles all 3 states (unsupported / denied / supported+permission default-or-granted) and properly toggles between Aktifkan / Nonaktifkan based on getPushState().subscribed. Good.

Hooks in _create_request, approve_request, reject_request, check_in(status=='late') call send_push_to_users which is now confirmed to be no-op-safe when there are no subscriptions — verified by employee create returning 200 and POST /api/requests returning <500.

Minor (non-blocking): /api/push/subscribe stores subscription doc with the entire 'subscription' payload (includes keys.p256dh/auth). That is required by pywebpush — acceptable. Consider adding TTL/cleanup for very old endpoints (>90 days) to prevent unbounded growth.
Minor: /api/push/unsubscribe always returns ok:true even when no matching doc was deleted. Acceptable (idempotent client-facing), but a count could help client debugging.

success_rate.backend: 100% (6/6 push tests pass; 21/21 pre-existing absensipro tests presumed unchanged)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on what is testable in headless (regression + UI shape). Real PushManager.subscribe flow NOT exercisable in headless Chromium — environment limitation, not an app defect.

seed_data_creation: None persistent — tests create + clean up fake subscription and TEST_-prefixed employees.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Push backend lifecycle is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_push.py (run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL set). Notes: (1) /api/push/vapid-public-key returned {key:'BIIG-oEWmNiZG1...', configured:true} — VAPID is configured in backend/.env. (2) Subscribe upsert is idempotent on 'endpoint' (Mongo unique index). (3) Unsubscribe scoped by endpoint+user_id. (4) For UI test of the toggle in headless Chromium, Notification.permission stays 'denied' even after context.grant_permissions(['notifications']) — verified empirically; toggle then shows 'Diblokir' which is the correct branch. To exercise the live subscribe flow you would need a real (non-headless) browser with a working push service (Chrome+FCM). All backend hooks (send_push_to_users) are no-op-safe when no subscriptions exist — confirmed by employee CRUD and request creation both completing without 5xx.
rca of the issue: No app issue. The only observation is environmental: headless Chromium does not honor grant_permissions(['notifications']) for Notification.permission and lacks a real push service, so the end-to-end PushManager.subscribe step cannot be executed in this automation. The application correctly degrades to the 'Diblokir' state under denied permission. Backend lifecycle is fully proven via pytest.
